entities.name: INTERNATIONAL AGRICULTURAL RESEARCH CENTRES
entities.description: Institutions expected to conduct research that produces International Public Goods with wide applicability and accessibility across many countries.

entities.name: ICRISAT
entities.description: The International Crops Research Institute for the Semi-Arid Tropics, an example of an International Agricultural Research Centre.

entities.name: INTERNATIONAL PUBLIC GOODS
entities.description: Technologies and research outputs with wide applicability and accessibility across many countries.

entities.name: LOCAL CONDITIONS
entities.description: Varying regional factors that agricultural technologies must be adapted to for successful adoption.

entities.name: CGIAR
entities.description: The Consultative Group on International Agricultural Research, providing the broader mandate for research focus.

entities.name: POVERTY ALLEVIATION
entities.description: A primary focus of the CGIAR mandate to address issues facing the poor.

entities.name: NATIONAL AGRICULTURAL RESEARCH SYSTEMS
entities.description: Research systems within developing countries that often have insufficient capacity.

entities.name: DEVELOPING COUNTRIES
entities.description: Regions where national agricultural research systems operate and where research for development is focused.

entities.name: RESEARCH FOR DEVELOPMENT
entities.description: Research engaged in by international institutions to address the range of issues facing the poor in different localities.

entities.name: PARTICIPATORY DOWNSTREAM RESEARCH
entities.description: Research focusing on local development agendas, sometimes criticized for placing emphasis on local needs at the expense of IPG delivery.

entities.name: LOCAL DEVELOPMENT AGENDAS
entities.description: Specific regional priorities emphasized in participatory downstream research.

entities.name: TECHNOLOGY DEVELOPMENT, ADAPTATION AND ADOPTION STUDIES
entities.description: Studies carried out at the local level within an IPG framework to ensure technology uptake.

entities.name: REVIEW OF LITERATURE
entities.description: A method used as background to complement discussions on IPGs and spillovers.

entities.name: IMPACT PATHWAYS ANALYSIS
entities.description: An analytical method used to complement discussions on IPGs and spillovers.

entities.name: SPILLOVERS
entities.description: The indirect effects or benefits resulting from the delivery of International Public Goods.

entities.name: DIFFUSION STRATEGIES
entities.description: Strategies used to increase the effectiveness of research output dissemination for ultimate impact.

entities.name: AGRICULTURAL DEVELOPMENT
entities.description: The ultimate goal achieved through effective research, diffusion strategies, and impact.

relationships.source: TECHNOLOGY DEVELOPMENT, ADAPTATION AND ADOPTION STUDIES
